name: R(D*) charged-current B-anomaly
aliases: B -> D* tau nu vs B -> D* mu nu
summary: The lepton-flavor-universality ratios R(D) and R(D*), where R(X) = BR(B -> X tau nu) / BR(B -> X mu nu), are measured by BaBar, Belle, Belle II, and LHCb to lie ~3.2 sigma above SM predictions. The charged-current process B -> D(*) tau nu would be modified at this level by a tree-level W' or scalar leptoquark coupling preferentially to the third lepton generation. The neutral-current R(K) anomaly resolved in 2022 (LHCb 2103.11769), but R(D*) tension remains, providing one of the cleanest flavor-physics data tensions.

characteristic_numbers.note: Combined deviation in (R(D), R(D*)) plane is ~3.2 sigma above SM.

principles_stressed.note: If real, requires new physics at ~ TeV with tau-vs-light-lepton non-universality. Scalar (S1, R2) or vector (U1) leptoquark models can fit, but face stringent LHC + flavor + EW-precision bounds. The LHCb-2024 R(D*) update is consistent with the anomaly. Active experimental program at Belle II + LHCb.

history: BaBar 2012 (arXiv:1205.5442) first hint of R(D*) excess. Belle 2017 (arXiv:1612.00529) confirmed; LHCb 2022-2023 added new measurements. HFLAV 2023 average: 3.2 sigma. R(K) anomaly was resolved in 2022 (LHCb arXiv:2212.09153), strengthening focus on charged-current side. Active interpretation programs: Crivellin-D'Ambrosio-Heeck-Hofer; Buttazzo-Greljo-Isidori-Marzocca minimal-leptoquark fits.

references.citation: HFLAV Collaboration, 'Averages of b-hadron, c-hadron, and tau-lepton properties as of 2021', Phys. Rev. D 107, 052008 (2023)
